svg.text: Pre-signed atomic transactions; lender pre-commits at origination; borrower repays unilaterally
— both parties cooperate, sign three transactions, store two off-chain

g.text: 0.35 → Bob (locked by sig — paired with Input 0)
↑ NOT covered by signatures — Alice can structure freely
Both parties verify the off-chain copies are stored, then sign & broadcast Tx-A
If the ceremony aborts before this point, no on-chain state is created

svg.text: — nothing happens on chain; both sides hold their pre-signed transactions

g.text: • Has 0.3 VRSC principal in her wallet
She can broadcast Tx-Repay any time before maturity
• Can only be moved via the 3 pre-signed txs
Or, with cooperation, by both parties signing a new tx
• Has nothing on chain to claim until maturity+grace
No live cooperation needed during the loan term
Alice signs new inputs/outputs with SIGHASH_ALL
Output 0 (Bob's payment) untouched — locked at origination
Lender's pre-sig remains valid — ANYONECANPAY ignores other inputs
She does this unilaterally — no live coordination with Bob
Alice's funding input: −0 VRSC (collateral covers it)
Tx-B becomes invalid — its input UTXO is consumed.
Bob never had to be online or sign anything at repayment time.
Loan term ends — Alice never broadcast Tx-Repay
Either she chose not to repay, or lost keys, or died
Bob retrieves Tx-B from storage and broadcasts it
Already fully signed by both parties from origination day
Bob receives: +0.3999 VRSC (entire collateral, less fee)
Tx-Repay becomes invalid — its input UTXO is consumed.
Alice never had to be online — Bob acted unilaterally.
She kept the 0.3 principal she borrowed; lost 0.5 collateral.
• Other inputs/outputs are FREE for borrower
• Same SIGHASH the marketplace uses internally
• Borrower can settle without lender online
• Held in Alice's wallet, never on chain
• No public offer in mempool to race
• When Alice broadcasts, the tx is complete
Output 0 is signed-locked to Bob's address:
• Force borrower to repay (default is her right)
• Resolve subjective disputes — those go to courts
• Recover from BOTH parties losing keys

svg.text: VerusLending v0.4 — github.com/Fried333/veruslending — empirically validated on Verus mainnet
